summary: [APU Health Monitor] Create query for APU Health Monitor in Engine Folder

output_fields.name: psn
output_fields.description: Unique PSN associated with each APU Serial Number

output_fields.name: ref_date
output_fields.description: Reference Date (latest date only for each APU)

transformations: Join rotables table with rotables_trend table on psn field
Join result with aircraft table on ac_registr field to filter by aircraft type
Filter rotables_trend records to include only the latest ref_date for each unique PSN
Retain event_perfno_i as-is, allowing NULL values for APUs without associated workorders
acceptance_criteria: Query returns all APUs of type B737NG with their latest CT5ATP trend data
Each APU appears only once with its most recent reference date
All required fields (partno, serialno, psn, ac_registr, ref_date, trend_type, event_perfno_i, value, ac_typ) are included in output
Workorder field (event_perfno_i) displays NULL when no workorder is associated with an APU
VDS is created in Engine Folder
Query executes successfully against AMOS Acceptance environment
Upon AMOS production go-live, data source can be switched to production without query logic changes
